original: Byzantine troops occupy Antioch (in modern Syria)
Beijing is declared the capital of the Ming dynasty under the Yongle Emperor
Christian I is crowned King of Denmark in the Church of Our Lady, Copenhagen, establishing the House of Oldenburg (still rules the Danish throne today)
Battle of Yaunis Khan: Turkish forces under the Grand Vizier Sinan Pasha defeat the Mameluks near Gaza
Battle of Amba Sel: Imam Ahmad ibn Ibrihim al-Ghazi again defeats the army of Lebna Dengel, Emperor of Ethiopia and gains control of the southern part of Ethiopia
The first university in the New World, the Universidad Santo Tomás de Aquino, is established on Hispaniola
Battle at Giurgevo: Zsigmond Bathory of Transylvania beats Turks
Japanese Keichō Embassy headed by Hasekura Tsunenaga leaves Sendai aboard the Japanese-built Date Maru galleon for Acapulco
Huguenot bulwark La Rochelle surrenders to Cardinal Richelieu
Harvard University, Cambridge, founded by a vote of the Massachusetts General Court
First Protestant church assembly for American Indians established in Massachusetts
The Duke of York and Albany's Maritime Regiment of Foot, later known as the Royal Marines, is established
St. Mary's Church is consecrated at Fort St George, Madras - now the oldest British building in India
Ivan VI becomes Tsar of Russia [OS=Oct 17]
The Peruvian cities of Lima and Callao are demolished by an earthquake, killing 18,000
New York gives up claims to Vermont for $30,000
Battle of Pinjarra occurs in the Swan River Colony in present-day Pinjarra, Western Australia; British colonists kill 14 to 40 Aboriginal people
Battle of Concepción: rebels led by James Bowie and James Fannin defeat Mexican troops at Mission Concepción, 2 miles (3.2 km) south of what is now San Antonio, Texas
Pioneers suffer blizzard in Sierra Nevada; 42 die
The first railroad in Spain opens, between Barcelona and Mataró
R. H. Macy & Co opens 1st store, (6th Ave-NYC) Gross receipts $11.06
Battle at Wauhatchie, Georgia: 865 killed or injured
Battle of Wauhatchie, Tennessee
Second Battle at Fair Oaks, Virginia, ends with 1554 casualties
Second Battle of Newtonia (American Civil War), Newton County, Missouri
Maimonides College in Pennsylvania is first Jewish college in the US
Athletics reveal $22,000 profit in their 1st season in the American Association
Quake strikes Mino-Owari, Japan kills 7,300
Mayor of Chicago Carter Harrison III is assassinated at home by a disgruntled supporter Patrick Eugene Prendergast
II Summer (Modern) Olympic Games in Paris end after five months; no opening or closing ceremonies are held
St. Louis police try a new investigative method - fingerprints
Belgian-British "Union Minière du Haut Katanga" mining company created in what is now the Democratic Republic of Congo
Bill Dobbie of Calgary Tigers kicks 10 singles in a game
German battle cruiser Goeben enters Black Sea
Omega Psi Phi Fraternity, founded at Howard University, incorporates
Australian government holds a national referendum seeking support for the proposal of compulsory conscription; it is rejected
Czechoslovakia gains independence as Austria-Hungary breaks up
Tomáš Massaryk claims independence for Czechoslovakia
Amsterdam's Tuschinski movie theater opens
First American gubernatorial recall election is held in North Dakota. Governor Lynn Frazier loses to Ragnvald A. Nestos by just over 4,000 votes (1.8%).
First US coast-to-coast radio broadcast of a football game
French-Russian trade agreement signed
White Sox beat NY Giants 8-4 in Dublin, less than 20 fans attend
KLM airmail plane "Homing Pigeon" arrives back in Amsterdam after the first special round trip flight to the Dutch East Indies
Indian freedom fighter Lala Lajpat Rai injured leading silent protest against visiting British commission in Lahore (dies of injuries 17th November)
Indonesian child laws enforced in Bahasa Indonesia
Dow Jones Industrial Average plummets 38.33 pts (13%) to 260.64
Brooklyn Dodgers and Pittsburgh Pirates play first of only 4 penalty free games in NFL history; Dodgers wins 21-3 at Ebbets Field, Brooklyn
Sidney Kingsley's play "Dead End" premieres in NYC
Farewell parade of International Brigade (Barcelona)
Anti-German demonstrations and strikes in Czechoslovakia
Spitfire shoots German Heinkel-111 down above Scotland
Empress of Britain sunk by a German U-boat two days after it was bombed off the west coast of Ireland with the lost of 49 lives. Largest Allied ship sunk in WWII.
Greece successfully resists Italy's attack
Meeting between Adolf Hitler andBenito Mussoliniin Florence, Italy
6th day battle at El Alamein: British offensive under Montgomery
Train crashes into bus, killing 16 & injuring 20 (Detroit Michigan)
German submarine U-220 sunk by US aircraft in the Atlantic
Russia & Bulgaria sign weapons pact
German rocket engineers begin work in USSR
Flag of Israel is adopted
Georges Bidault elected president of France
US performs nuclear test at Nevada Test Site
Bud Grant of CFL Winnipeg Blue Bombers intercepts 5 passes (record)
Red Barber, resigns as Dodger sportscaster to join Yankees
Major league owners vote down sale of A's to a Philadelphia syndicate
N. Richard Nash's play "The Rainmaker" premieres in NYC
Egypt and Saudi Arabia sign defense treaty
WMVS TV channel 10 in Milwaukee, WI (PBS) begins broadcasting
Pete Runnels wins Comeback Player of Year (avg went from .230 to .322)
American Football League awards Buffalo Bills franchise to Ralph C Wilson
"Fiorello!" closes at the Broadhurst Theatre in NYC, starring Tom Bosley, after 796 performances and a Pulitzer Prize
Ground broken on the Municipal (Shea) Stadium in Queens, NY, home of the NY Mets until 2008
NY Giant Y. A. Tittle passes for 7 touchdowns vs Wash Redskins (49-34)
Radio Moscow reports nuclear missiles in Cuba deactivated
USSR performs nuclear test at the Semipalatinsk Test Site in northeast Kazakhstan
Gateway Arch (630' (190m) high) completed in St Louis, Missouri
Belgium's Gaston Roelants runs 12-4/5 miles in 1 hour
NBA Cleveland Cavaliers 1st home game, lose to San Diego 110-99
US/USSR sign an agreement to discuss joint space efforts
United Kingdom becomes the 6th nation to have a satellite in orbit with the launch of Prospero
Elmore Smith of Lakers blocks 17 shots in a game (NBA record)
1st time 2 Islanders hat trick in same game-MacMillian & Westfall
Luna 23 launched (landing on Moon)
US performs nuclear test at Nevada Test Site
Calvin Murphy (Houston) begins NBA free throw streak of 58 games
Cleveland Metroparks assume operating responsibilities for Zoo (Cleveland, Ohio)
Venezuela and foreign oil companies agree on nationalization as of January 1, 1976
Don Ritchie runs world record 100k (6:10:20)
NBC's premiere of Kiss' acting debut, "Kiss Meets The Phantom of the Park" TV film
Dick Howser (best Yank manager win-lost pct .636) returns to New York, replacingBilly Martin
USSR performs nuclear test at the Semipalatinsk Test Site in northeast Kazakhstan
Edward M McIntrye elected 1st black mayor of Augusta Georgia
NASA launches RCA-E
Spain's socialists win/communists lose elections
Ravi Ratnayeke takes 8-83 for Sri Lanka v Pakistan
Stanley E. Hubbard's KOB sells the company's Albuquerque, New Mexico radio stations, KOB-AM and KOB-FM, the stations change their call signs to KKOB
The centennial of the Statue of Liberty's dedication is celebrated in New York Harbor
Jurors award $147,000 to Tacoma parishioner seduced by her minister
Lee Jang Rim predicts that today would be the end of the world!
Cleveland Metroparks lease Brookside Park from Cleveland for 99-years
Dutch Antilles government of Liberia-Peters resigns
Japanese space probe Sakigake passes Earth for 3rd time

Apple Records releases The Beatles "Anthology 3" (double CD/triple LP)", the last of 3-part series of rare recordings and outtakes by the Beatles; set covers the years 1968-70; album tops Billboard charts in US and peaks at no. 4 in UK
Goa upset Karnataka to win their 1st Ranji Cricket Trophy game ever
NBA announces hiring of Dee Kantner & Violet Palmer as 1st women to officiate a major-league all-male sports league
An Air China (Mainland China) jetliner is hijacked by disgruntled pilot Yuan Bin and flown to Taiwan.
Plame affair: Lewis Libby, Vice-president Dick Cheney's chief of staff, is indicted in the Valerie Plame case. Libby resigns later that day.

Funeral service for the peace of the executed at Bykivnia forest, outside of Kyiv, Ukraine, with reburial of 817 Ukrainian civilians (out of some 100,000) executed by Bolsheviks at Bykivnia in 1930s - early 1940s.
"Bee Movie" premieres in the US and the UK
In the UK, 500,000 mortgage holders are left in negative equity after house prices drop 15% since the previous summer
NASA successfully launches the Ares I-X mission, the only rocket launch for its later-cancelled Constellation program.
The 28 October 2009 Peshawar bombing kills 117 and wounds 213.
15 people are killed and 33 injured by a series of Baghdad car bombings
Syrian ceasefire collapses and 128 people are killed in ongoing civil war violence
Research indicating the Plague dates back to the Bronze Age in skeletons 5,783 years old, published by a University of Copenhagen team in "Cell"
Third Republican presidential candidates debate run by CNBC in Boulder, Colorado
World Heath Organization ranks Tuberculosis alongside HIV as world's deadliest infectious diseases, killing 1.2 million (2014)
Ross Sea Region Marine Protected Area established off Antarctica - world's largest marine protected area at 598,000 square miles (2.06 million square-kilometers), larger than Mexico
Twin car bomb attacks in Mogadishu, Somalia, kill at least 27, Islamist militant group al-Shabab claim responsibility
Michael D. Higgins is re-elected Irish president
Political crisis in Sri Lanka after President Sirisena sacks Prime Minister Ranil Wickramasinghe and the cabinet, suspends parliament for two weeks with one man killed in protests
Ukrainian tennis star Elina Svitolina registers biggest win of her career when she rallies for a 3-6, 6-2, 6-2 triumph over American Sloane Stephens in a classic WTA Finals title decider in Singapore
Country of Georgia hit by a massive cyber attack affecting 2,000 websites, TV channels and government departments.
EU agrees to another Brexit extension to January 31 2020
Landslide after heavy rains in Bafoussam, Cameroon, buries at least 42 people
15th-century medieval manuscript "The Book of Lismore", returns to Ireland after donated by Chatsworth Settlement to University College Cork
Global COVID-19 cases record one-day increase of more than 500,000 for the first time, rising 25% in under two weeks according to Reuters
Hurricane Zeta makes landfall in the US near Cocodrie, Louisiana, as category 2 storm
New coral reef 500m (1,640ft) high, taller than the Empire State Building, discovered north of Australia's Great Barrier Reef
Tanzania reelects President John Magufuli, with opposition parties calling the vote fraudulent
Typhoon Molave strikes Vietnam triggering heavy rain and landslides, leaving more than 60 people dead
Largest-ever drug bust in Asia made by police in Laos with 55 million methamphetamine tablets and 1.5 tonnes of crystal meth discovered in beer crates
One of world's largest floor mosaics unveiled after restoration at Hisham's Palace (660-750 AD) by Palestinian authorities in Jericho, West Bank
Researchers announce name of a new human ancestor, Homo bodoensis, from Africa during the Middle Pleistocene, about half a million years ago, the direct ancestor of modern humans
Swedish engineers in Linköping produce the world's first female crash dummy (previous female dummies just a scaled down version of the male figure)
Tropical storm Nalgae stikes makes landfall in Virac, the Philippines, will go on to kill 112 people in the country

10th Rugby World Cup, Stade de France: South Africa become first to win 4 World Cups and second to go back-to-back, after gripping 12-11 win over New Zealand; MVP: Springboks flanker ﻿Pieter-Steph du Toit
FIFA Ballon d'Or: Spain and Man City midfielder Rodri wins men's award from Real Madrid pair Vinicius Jr and Jude Bellingham; Barcelona's Aitana Bonmati wins back-to-back women's awards
UK records its lowest-ever fertility rate of 1.44 children per woman in 2023, with 591,072 births in England and Wales, the lowest in 172 years
Maxentiusproclaimed Emperor of Rome
EmperorConstantine the Greatdefeats rivalMaxentiusat the Battle of the Milvian Bridge
Holy Roman EmperorHenry IVdeposes the Bishop of Parma and Antipope Honorius II
Henry III, aged nine, is crowned King of England at Gloucester Cathedral after the death of his father,King John; a second coronation is held in 1220
Charles V's son succeeds him as KingCharles VI of France
Battle at Brustem: Duke of BurgundyCharles the Bolddefeats Prince-Bishopric of Liège near Sint-Truiden, modern-day Belgium
Christopher Columbussights Cuba and claims it for Spain under the name "Juana"
BritishKing James Iappoints musician Robert Dowland court lutenist
English KingCharles Isigns a peace treaty with the Scottish Covenanters, ending the Second Bishops' War
"Gulliver's Travels" byJonathan Swiftis published by Benjamin Motte in London
Battle of White Plains:George Washingtonretreats to New Jersey
Eli Whitneyapplies for a patent for his cotton gin machine, used to pull cotton fibers from the seed
First known purchase ofJane Austen's novel "Sense and Sensibility" by the Prince Regent (later George IV), a fan
Clara Wieck, age 9, makes her official debut in a piano recital at the Gewandhaus concert hall in Leipzig, Germany
French MagicianJean Eugène Robert-Houdinperforms for Algerian Marabouts in Algeria to convince them that French magic is the stronger
Pyotr Ilyich Tchaikovskyconducts first performance of his Symphony Number Six in B minor, "Pathetique", in St. Petersburg, Russia, 9 days before his death
Richard Strauss' tone poem "Eine Alpensinfonie (An Alpine Symphony)" premieres in Berlin
Republic of Czechoslovakia created withTomáš Masarykas its 1st president
Volstead Act is passed by Congress, establishing prohibition despite PresidentWoodrow Wilson's veto
30, 000 Italian fascists conduct the 'March on Rome', leading to the assumption of power byBenito Mussolini

Miner M.de Bruin discovers the infant fossil skull, "Taung child" in a lime quarry in Taung, South Africa. PaleoanthropologistRaymond Dartidentifies the fossil as a new hominin species, Australopithecus africanus.
Josip Broz Titobegins 7 months jail sentence in Croatia
FDRrededicates Statue of Liberty on its 50th anniversary
Warner Bros, releases gangster film "The Roaring Twenties", directed by Raoul Walsh, starringJames CagneyandHumphrey Bogart
"How Green Was My Valley" based on the novel by Richard Llewellyn, directed byJohn Fordand starring Walter Pidgeon andMaureen O'Harapremieres in New York (Best Picture 1942)
Juan Manuel Fangioof Argentina wins Formula 1 World Drivers Championship by taking out the Spanish Grand Prix at Pedralbes in an Alpha Romeo; wins by 6 points fromAlberto Ascariof Italy
Nobel Prize for Literature is awarded toErnest Hemingway
PopePius XIIpublishes encyclical Luctuosissimi eventus
Angelo Giuseppe Roncalli elected Pope, taking nameJohn XXIII
Jean Genet's play "Les Negres" premieres in Paris
PopePaul VIproclaims Jews not collectively guilty for crucifixion
John LennonandYoko Onorecord the single "Happy Xmas (War is Over)" in NYC
Indian Institute of Management Bangalore, designed by architect B. V. Doshi, inaugurated by Prime MinisterIndira Gandhi
Billy Martinnamed AL Manager of Year (NY Yankees)
Bobby Orrscores his last career NHL goal (vs Detroit Red Wings)
Microsoft co-founderPaul Allengives $10 million to University of Washington library

Columbia Records releases "Merry Christmas", the fourth studio album by American singer-songwriterMariah Careyfeaturing a mix of new and traditional holiday songs; global smash features the hit single "All I Want for Christmas is You" goes on to sell 9 million units to date
Indie cult hit film "Donnie Darko", starringJake Gyllenhaal, is released
AmericanSerena Williamswins her 3rd season ending tennis title; beatsMaria Sharapovaof Russia 6–4, 6–3 in the WTA Championship decider in Istanbul, Turkey
Disney Records releasesAlessia Cara's single "How Far I'll Go" from the film "Moana"
Spain's central government imposes direct rule onCatalonia, dismisses its government and calls for new elections in December
British Mercedes driverLewis Hamiltonfinishes 4th in the Mexican Grand Prix at Autódromo Hermanos Rodríguez to clinch his 5th Formula 1 World Drivers Championship
French PresidentEmmanuel Macronannounces France will enter a new 4-week lockdown October 30 in televised address
Mark Zuckerbergannounces Facebook will change its corporate name to Meta amid increased public scrutiny over leaked internal documents
Hammer-wielding intruder attacks Paul Pelosi, 82-year old husband of US Speaker of the HouseNancy Pelosi, at their California home
Michael Faradaydemonstrates his dynamo invention, an electrical generator
Statue of Liberty is dedicated by PresidentGrover Cleveland, celebrated by the first confetti (ticker tape) parade in New York City
Cuban Missile Crisis: US President JFK receives letter from Soviet LeaderrKhrushchevsuggesting agreement
The British Parliament debates the European Communities principle of membership and votes 356 to 244 in favour of joining, requiring a new law to be drafted and a later final vote on joining
